revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)-qq]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=draft}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=draft}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=draft}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]
