{{神社 |名称 = 瀧尾神社 |画像 = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
本殿正面 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[東山区]]本町11丁目718 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 58 | 緯度秒 = 57.221 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 14.301 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city2 | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[大国主神]]
[[弁財天]]
[[毘沙門天]] |社格 = [[近代社格制度|村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 多景の社(旧称) |例祭 = 神幸祭（9月最終土曜日） |神事 = }} '''瀧尾神社'''（たきおじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[東山区]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== *[[大国主神]]（おおくにぬしのかみ） *[[弁財天]]（べんざいてん） *[[毘沙門天]]（びしゃもんてん） == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳であるが、平安末期に書かれた『[[源平盛衰記]]』には「武鶏ノ社」という記述があることから、この頃には存在したと考えられる[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html 瀧尾神社 - ちょっと言いたくなる 京都通 ‐ 宇治茶 伊藤久右衛門]2018年4月15日　閲覧。しかし、[[応仁の乱]]によって焼失し、吉坂に移って多景の社と改称して鎮座していた[http://kyototravel.info/takiojinjya 瀧尾神社 - 京都観光・旅行]2018年4月15日　閲覧。1586年（[[天正]]14年）10月に、[[豊臣秀吉]]が[[方広寺]]大仏殿([[京の大仏]])を建立したことに伴って現在地に遷座した。その後、[[宝永]]年間に江戸幕府の命令によって改築され、社号も多景の社から現在の瀧尾神社と改称した。本殿は[[貴船神社]]奥院旧殿を移築したもので、拝殿など計8棟が京都市指定[[有形文化財]]である。本殿前の京都市による由緒書きに拠る。また、[[泉涌寺]]の僧の守るところであったともされる。 === 大丸との関係 === 瀧尾神社は2018年現在まで[[大丸]]との関係が深い。これは創業者である下村彦右衛門正啓が自宅のあった伏見京町から行商へ行く道中にあった神社に毎朝欠かさず参拝していたことから由来しており、後に大丸が繁栄したことから代々下村家より崇敬されてきたためである[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ HIGASHIYAMA district ｜ 京都 東山区 瀧尾神社 （たきおじんじゃ）]2018年4月15日　閲覧。1839年（[[天保]]10年）から翌年にかけて、下村家が2500両（現在の貨幣価値で約5億円）をかけて本殿、拝殿、手水舎、絵馬舎が整備された。 == 境内 == 拝殿の天井には、江戸時代後期の彫物師である九山新太郎が製作した全長8ｍに及ぶ龍の木彫り像が飾られている。彫物師の九山家は当主が代々九山新之丞を名乗り、その一派は[[祇園祭]]に登場する[[大船鉾]]の龍頭も手がけたとされており、[[禁門の変]]で焼失した大船鉾を2016年に再現した際には、現・九山新之丞が船首の龍頭のモデルとして瀧尾神社の龍を参考に用いた[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ 龍の彫刻がスゴイ！京都のパワースポット「瀧尾神社」- トラベルジェイピー]2018年4月15日　閲覧。 この龍の木彫り像はあまりにも精密に作られていたために、現地住民の間で「夜になると龍が動き出して水を飲みに行く」という噂が立ったため、神社によって逃げ出さないように金網が敷かれていたことがある（現在は金網が撤去されている）[https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/8316　瀧尾神社 | 京都の観光スポット | 京都観光情報 KYOTOdesign]2018年4月15日　閲覧。 == 摂末社 == *金刀比羅宮 （祭神：[[崇徳天皇]]） *瀧尾天満宮（祭神：[[菅原道真]]） *大丸繁栄稲荷 （祭神：[[稲荷大神]]） *愛宕神社 （祭神：愛宕大神） *妙見宮 （祭神：妙見大明神） *三嶋神社 （祭神：[[大山祇命]]、[[木之花咲耶姫]]、[[瓊々杵尊]]） :例年10月には三嶋神社の眷属がウナギとされているため、三嶋神社の祈願所で鰻放生大祭が行われる *三社 :門出社 （祭神：不明） :朝日社 （祭神：不明） :天満宮 （祭神：菅原道真） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[京都府]][[京都市]][[東山区]]本町11丁目718 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[奈良線]]及び[[京阪本線]][[東福寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[福助]] - 前述の下村彦右衛門がモデルとされており、瀧尾神社でも近隣の人形師によって作られた福助人形を授与品として頒布している。 *昇龍会　神輿 *誠龍会　奉賛　会長 奥田成親 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきおしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:東山区の建築物]] [[Category:東山区の歴史]] [[Category:大丸松坂屋百貨店|たきおしんしや]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]]

It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:大丸松坂屋百貨店]] [[Category:東山区の建築物]] [[Category:東山区の歴史]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]]

Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:大丸松坂屋百貨店]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:東山区の歴史]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]]

Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:大丸松坂屋百貨店]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama Ward]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores�]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines of the Shinto Honkyō Sect]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[Nara Line]] and [[Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Takio-jinja2.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the main shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[:en:The Tale of the Heike|The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}

The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[:en:Tōfukuji Station|Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Taki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:aki-no-o Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}}
