It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[:en:The Tale of the Heike|The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[:en:Tōfukuji Station|Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}}

It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[:en:The Tale of the Heike|The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[:en:Tōfukuji Station|Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}}

It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[:en:The Tale of the Heike|The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[:en:Tōfukuji Station|Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Takio-jinja]][[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takio-jinja]][[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q54153288]] Wikidata name: Takio Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takio-jinja2.jpg | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = | prefecture= {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.98256139|N|135.77063917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[:en:The Tale of the Heike|The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[:en:Tōfukuji Station|Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Takio-jinja]][[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[commons:Category:Takio-jinja]][[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takio-jinja]][[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q54153288]] Wikidata name: Takio Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takio-jinja2.jpg | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = | prefecture= {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.98256139|N|135.77063917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''[[:en:The Tale of the Heike|The Tale of the Heike]]'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]]. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to [[:en:Daimaru|Daimaru]], dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari Ōkami]]) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and [[:en:Keihan Main Line|Keihan Main Line]] [[:en:Tōfukuji Station|Tōfukuji Station]] (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[:en:Fukusuke|Fukusuke]] - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Address * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|Tōfukuji Station|en|Tōfukuji Station|WD=Q1044897|lt=Tōfukuji Station}} (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Fukusuke|en|Fukusuke|WD=Q5507747|lt=Fukusuke}} - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Takio-jinja]][[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Takio-jinja]][[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takio-jinja]][[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q54153288]] Wikidata name: Takio Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Taki-o Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Takio-jinja2.jpg | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 | Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 58 | LatSeconds = 57.221 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | LonMinutes = 46 | LonSeconds = 14.301 | E/W = E | MapCode = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | LabelPosition = left | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Benzaiten]]
[[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = | prefecture= {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.98256139|N|135.77063917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''{{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=The Tale of the Heike}}'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}} and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei}} era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyū-ji}}. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to {{ill|Daimaru|en|Daimaru|WD=Q3012112|lt=Daimaru}}, dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami}}) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|Tōfukuji Station|en|Tōfukuji Station|WD=Q1044897|lt=Tōfukuji Station}} (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Fukusuke|en|Fukusuke|WD=Q5507747|lt=Fukusuke}} - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = | prefecture= {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.98256139|N|135.77063917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''{{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=The Tale of the Heike}}'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}} and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei}} era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyū-ji}}. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to {{ill|Daimaru|en|Daimaru|WD=Q3012112|lt=Daimaru}}, dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami}}) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|Tōfukuji Station|en|Tōfukuji Station|WD=Q1044897|lt=Tōfukuji Station}} (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Fukusuke|en|Fukusuke|WD=Q5507747|lt=Fukusuke}} - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It is also said to have been under the care of monks from {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyū-ji|1=Sennyū-ji}}. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to {{ill|Daimaru|en|Daimaru|WD=Q3012112|lt=Daimaru|1=Daimaru}}, dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}}) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|Tōfukuji Station|en|Tōfukuji Station|WD=Q1044897|lt=Tōfukuji Station|1=Tōfukuji Station}} (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Fukusuke|en|Fukusuke|WD=Q5507747|lt=Fukusuke|1=Fukusuke}} - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[Benzaiten]]
[[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] | Status = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Village shrine]] | consecration_year = Unknown | MainHall = | Alias = Shrine of Many Views (old name) | Festival = Shinkōsai (last Saturday of September) | Ceremonies = | prefecture= {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.98256139|N|135.77063917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Taki-o Shrine''' (瀧尾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]]. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Village shrine|village shrine]] under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] (Ōkuninushi no Kami) * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] == History == The foundation date of Taki-o Shrine is unknown, but the shrine is mentioned in the late Heian period work, ''{{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=The Tale of the Heike|1=The Tale of the Heike}}'', as "Take-no-Yashiro" (武鶏ノ社), suggesting it existed by this time.[https://www.itohkyuemon.co.jp/site/kyoto/tuu161.html Taki-o Shrine - Itoh Kyuemon's Kyoto Travel Guide] Accessed April 15, 2018. The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}} and was relocated to Yoshizaka, where it was renamed the Shrine of Many Views. In October 1586 ([[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 14), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} moved the shrine to its current location to make way for the construction of the {{ill|8=ja|9=京の大仏|Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}} era, the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]] rebuilt the shrine and renamed it Taki-o Shrine. The main hall, relocated from the inner shrine of [[Kifune Shrine]], and other structures were designated [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[Tangible Cultural Properties]].Based on the historical marker by Kyoto City. It is also said to have been under the care of monks from {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyū-ji|1=Sennyū-ji}}. === Connection with Daimaru === Taki-o Shrine has long-standing ties to {{ill|Daimaru|en|Daimaru|WD=Q3012112|lt=Daimaru|1=Daimaru}}, dating back to its founder, Shimomura Hikoemon Masaaki, who visited the shrine daily while traveling for trade from his home in Fushimi Kyomachi. The shrine has been venerated by the Shimomura family, who credited it for Daimaru's prosperity.[http://kyoto-higashiyama.jp/shrinestemples/takiojinjya/ Taki-o Shrine - Higashiyama District] Accessed April 15, 2018. In 1839 ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 10), the Shimomura family spent 2,500 ryō (equivalent to approximately 500 million yen today) to renovate the shrine, including the main hall, prayer hall, ablution pavilion, and ema pavilion. == Grounds == The prayer hall's ceiling features an 8-meter-long wooden dragon sculpture by Edo-period artisan Kuzan Shintarō. The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}}) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ; Address * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Honmachi 11-chome 718 ; Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] and {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|WD=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|Tōfukuji Station|en|Tōfukuji Station|WD=Q1044897|lt=Tōfukuji Station|1=Tōfukuji Station}} (approximately 3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Fukusuke|en|Fukusuke|WD=Q5507747|lt=Fukusuke|1=Fukusuke}} - A figurine modeled after Shimomura Hikoemon, distributed as a talisman at the shrine. * Shōryūkai Mikoshi * Seiryūkai Support Society President Okuda Narichika {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|瀧尾神社 (京都市)|version=100386538|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:瀧尾神社 (京都市)]] {{Commons category|Takio-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q54153288}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Daimaru Matsuzakaya Department Stores]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The Kuzan family, famous for crafting [[Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] floats, created this sculpture so precisely that local rumors claimed the dragon moved to drink water at night, leading the shrine to install a wire mesh over it to prevent its escape (later removed).[https://www.travel.co.jp/guide/article/30882/ Taki-o Shrine: Kyoto's Power Spot] Accessed April 15, 2018. == Sub-shrines == * Konpira Shrine (deity: {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}) * Taki-o Tenmangū (deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) * Daimaru Prosperity Inari (deity: {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}}) * Atago Shrine (deity: Atago Ōkami) * Myōken Shrine (deity: Myōken Daibosatsu) * Mishima Shrine (Deities: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]) : Annual eel release festival due to the deity's connection to eels. * Sansha : Kadode Shrine (deity unknown) : Asahi Shrine (deity unknown) : Tenmangū (deity: Sugawara no Michizane) == Information == ;
